# fix(distributed): validate Worker dequeue execution_limits + grace_seconds (#912 Shard 6)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `793b0fdafd9e` — fix(distributed): validate Worker dequeue execution_limits + grace_seconds (#912 Shard 6)

**Body**:

The /redteam Round 1 audit caught two security findings the in-process
fix doesn't cover:

F2 (Worker accepts arbitrary execution_limits dict shape):
  A malicious or mis-coded producer can send arbitrary shapes on the
  wire — `{"soft": "DROP TABLE"}`, `{"soft": [1, 2, 3]}`, `{"hard": -1}`.
  Without dequeue-side validation, the bad value flows to
  arm_time_limits where it raises TypeError / ValueError from a daemon
  thread — far from the entry point with no actionable traceback.

  Fix: `_validate_execution_limits_dict` static helper called from
  `_effective_time_limits` at dequeue. Validates:
  - dict-or-None (rejects strings, lists, integers)
  - "soft" / "hard" values are int/float (NOT bool — Python's bool is
    a subclass of int, but `True` as a 1-second time limit is nonsense)
  - delegates to `_validate_limits` for finite-check + sign + ordering
  - silently ignores unknown keys for forward-compat

  When validation fails, `_effective_time_limits` raises ValueError;
  the existing broad `except Exception` in `_execute_task` catches it
  and dead-letters the malformed task with the actionable error
  message stored in TaskResult.error.

F3 (grace_seconds was unvalidated):
  Worker(hard_time_limit_grace_seconds=-1) makes the hard kill fire
  BEFORE the soft signal, inverting the celery contract.
  Worker(...=NaN) crashes the daemon thread at fire time.

  Fix: forward `grace_seconds` to `_validate_limits` from the Worker
  __init__ validator, so bad operator config raises at construction.

13 new Tier-2 tests cover every malicious shape class + the forward-
compat path + the no-override common path. Tests do NOT need Redis —
they exercise the validation surface directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
